content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 2, 1989

The Honorable Judith A. Nieburger
Councilwoman
City of Moreno Valley
P.O. Box 1440
Moreno Valley, CA  92337

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-027

Dear Councilwoman Nieburger:

     You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ I have
provided answers to your questions in a telephone conversation on
Tuesday, February 21, 1989. This letter is written confirmation of
the telephone advice.

QUESTIONS

     1.  May you use campaign funds in your possession prior to
January 1, 1989 for a reelection campaign in 1992?

     2.  May you solicit or receive campaign funds be starting im-
mediately for a reelection campaign in 1992?

     3.  For what purposes may you expend campaign funds collected
before January 1, 1989?

     4.  May you receive contributions after January 1, 1989 for
expenses associated with your current officeholder
responsibilities?

CONCLUSIONS

     1.  You may not use campaign funds in your possession prior
to January 1, 1989 for a reelection campaign in 1992.

_______________________
¹Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Judith A. Nieburger
Page Two

     2. You may solicit or receive campaign funds to be used for
a campaign in 1992 at any time after properly filing a notice
of intention to be a candidate for the specific office.

     3. You may use campaign funds collected before January 1,
1989 for officeholder expenses which are not campaign expenses for
a future campaign and are not "mass mailings."

     4. You may receive contributions after January 1, 1989 for
expenses associated with your current officeholder
responsibilities.

FACTS

     You are a current city councilperson. Your last election was
in 1988. You have campaign funds of approximately $1,600 which
were received in 1988 and were in your possession on January 1,
1989.

ANALYSIS

     Proposition 73, approved by the voters at the June 7, 1988
state primary election, imposes limitations and other requirements
on candidates for California state or local office and on persons,
political committees, and political parties which contribute to
such candidates. The contribution limitations apply contributions
received commencing January 1, 1989. Restrictions on the use of
the use of funds apply to funds held prior to January 1, 1988.
Your specific questions are discussed below.

Question 1.

     No campaign funds which were received prior to January 1,
1989 may be used to support or to oppose a candidacy. (Section
85306.) Therefore, you may not use any campaign funds in your
possession prior to January 1, 1989 to support your reelection
campaign in 1992.2


___________________________
2A recent decision by the Los Angeles Superior Court invalidated a
Commission regulation which permitted the use of campaign funds,
under certain circumstances, collected before January 1, 1989.
(Calif. Common Cause v. Calif. Fair Political Practices Comm'n,
Case No. C709383.) New litigation or further Commission decisions
may alter this advice in the future.

Judith A. Nieburger
Page Three

Question 2.

You asked about soliciting or receiving new funds after
January 1, 1989 for your 1992 election campaign. Proposition 73
imposes no time limits on campaign fundraising. There are various
procedural requirements, however.

All individuals, including current officeholders, who intend
to seek another term of office or to seek California state or
local office, must file with the Commission a statement of
intention to be a candidate for a specific office (Form 501).
(Section 85200.) If you receive $1,000 or more in contributions,
including your personal funds which you intend to use to support
your candidacy, you must file a statement of organization, Form
410, to create a recipient committee. (Section 84101.) All
campaign funds received for the specific office indicated on the
statement of intention must be deposited into a single campaign
bank account from which all campaign expenditures must be made.
(Section 85201.) You are required to file a candidate bank
account statement (Form 502) when you open the bank account.

Question 3.

Funds which you collected prior to January 1, 1989 may be
used for expenses associated with holding your office, other than
expenses for mass mailings and other campaign related activities.
(Regulation 18536.2, copy enclosed.) These funds, known as
"restricted funds," also may be used to pay past campaign debts to
make contributions to candidates for federal office or elected
office in a state other than California or to any ballot measure
other than a recall measure. (Regulation 18536.2.)

Question 4.

You may receive contributions after January 1, 1989 for
expenses associated with holding your current office. However,
prior to soliciting or receiving any contribution for this or any
other purpose, you must file a statement of intention (Form 501)
in which you identify your 1988 election as the office of intention.
You must have an account for these funds which is separate
from the restricted funds which are discussed above. (Section
85201.) You are required to file a candidate bank account form
(Form 502) when you open the bank account.

Judith A. Nieburger
Page Four


If you have further questions, please feel free to call be me
at (916) 322-5662.


Sincerely,


Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Bruce W. Robeck
Political Reform Consultant

City of Moreno Valley
PO Box 1440, Moreno Valley, CA 92337

City Council
Offices                January 6, 1989

City Hall
23119 Cottonwood
Building B
P.O. Box 1440
Moreno Valley, CA
(714) 243-3008
FAX: (714) 243-3009

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

RE:  REQUEST FOR WRITTEN ADVICE

To whom it may concern:

The undersigned is a member of the City Council of the
City of Moreno Valley, having been elected to the Council
in November of 1984 and re-elected in November of 1988 to
a four-year term expiring in November of 1992.

In connection with the 1988 City Council election, I accepted
campaign contributions intended to support my re-election, all
of which were deposited into one campaign bank account.  All
contributions were received and deposited between September 1,
1988 and December 30, 1988.

Except for approximately $1,600.00, all contributions were
duly expended for permitted campaign purposes.  With respect
to the unexpended balance and future contributions, I request
that you advise me regarding the following questions:

1.  May I carry over all or part of the 1988 balance
    to be used in support of my re-election to the
    City Council in 1992?

2.  May I accept, retain and expend contributions for
    the 1992 campaign in the interim between the present
    and actually declaring for re-election in 1992?

3.  If I may carry over the unexpended balance, or if I
    may accept further contributions before actually
    declaring for re-election, or if both are permissible,
    for what purposes may such funds be expended?

Page 2
January 6, 1988
Re: Request for Written Advice

4. In respect to the unexpended present balance
only, it is my intent to make the following
charitable disbursements therefrom:

a) $200 to the Moreno Valley Christmas Assist-
ance Program, a non-profit association pro-
viding food, shelter and clothing to needy
persons;

b) $300 to the Valley Animal Shelter, a humane
society facility;

c) $300 to the Friends of the Moreno Valley
Seniors Center, a non-profit group providing
assistance to senior citizens; and

d) $50 to Future Business Leaders, a non-profit
group associated with Canyon Springs High School
of the Moreno Valley Unified School District,
providing career guidance assistance to high
school students.

Are the above-listed proposed expenditures of campaign contributions
legally permissible?

5. It is my intent to establish an ad hoc advisory committee,
the membership of which will be open to anyone who wishes
to attend the meetings. The purpose of the committee, and
its meetings, will be to allow the members to comment upon
and advise me in regard to political and social issues and
problems arising within my councilmanic district and in the
city at-large. In respect to the unexpended balance with
campaign contributions as referenced above, may I expend
such funds: (1) to pay for publication of notices of
meetings and (2) to pay for food and refreshments to be
served at the meetings?

6. May I accept contributions after January 1, 1989 if they
are deposited in the account I have and can the funds be
used in the discharge of my duties in the office I currently
hold?

Please communicate with me at the above address and telephone number if there
are any questions or if more information is desired.

Very truly yours,

Judith A. Nieburger
Councilwoman

89-0224

City of Moreno Valley
P.O. Box 1440, Moreno Valley, CA 92337

City Council
Offices

January 6, 1989

City Hall
23119 Cottonwood
Building B
P.O. Box 1440
Moreno Valley, CA
(714) 243-3008
FAX:(714) 243-3009

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

RE: REQUEST FOR WRITTEN ADVICE

To whom it may concern:

The undersigned is a member of the City Council of the
City of Moreno Valley, having been elected to the Council
in November of 1984 and re-elected in November of 1988 to
a four-year term expiring in November of 1992.

In connection with the 1988 City Council election, I accepted
campaign contributions intended to support my re-election, all
of which were deposited into one campaign bank account. All
contributions were received and deposited between September 1,
1988 and December 30, 1988.

Except for approximately $1,600.00, all contributions were
duly expended for permitted campaign purposes. With respect

1. May I carry over all or part of the 1988 balance

City Council in 1992?

2. May I accept, retain and expend contributions for
the 1992 campaign in the interim between the present
and actually declaring for re-election in 1992?

3. If I may carry over the unexpended balance, or if I
may accept further contributions before actually
declaring for re-election, or if both are permissible,
for what purposes may such funds be expended?

Page 2
January 6, 1988
Re: Request for Written Advice

4. In respect to the unexpended present balance
only, it is my intent to make the following
charitable disbursements therefrom:

a) $200 to the Moreno Valley Christmas Assist-
ance Program, a non-profit association pro-
viding food, shelter and clothing to needy
persons;

b) $300 to the Valley Animal Shelter, a humane
society facility;

c) $300 to the Friends of the Moreno Valley
Seniors Center, a non-profit group providing
assistance to senior citizens; and

d) $50 to Future Business Leaders, a non-profit
group associated with Canyon Springs High School
of the Moreno Valley Unified School District,
providing career guidance assistance to high
school students.

Are the above-listed proposed expenditures of campaign contributions
legally permissible?

5. It is my intent to establish an ad hoc advisory committee,
the membership of which will be open to anyone who wishes
to attend the meetings. The purpose of the committee, and
its meetings, will be to allow the members to comment upon
and advise me in regard to political and social issues and

city at-large. In respect to the unexpended balance with
campaign contributions as referenced above, may I expend
such funds: (1) to pay for publication of notices of
meetings and (2) to pay for food and refreshments to be
served at the meetings?

6. May I accept contributions after January 1, 1989 if they
are deposited in the account I have and can the funds be
used in the discharge of my duties in the office I currently
hold?

Please communicate with me at the above address and telephone number if there
are any questions or if more information is desired.

Very truly yours,

Judith A. Nieburger
Councilwoman

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 20, 1989


Honorable Judith A. Neiburger
Councilmember
P.O. Box 1440
Moreno Valley, CA  92337

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-027

Dear Councilmember Neiburger:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on January 13, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief Technical Assistance
                                        and Analysis Division
